question_text: A mobile phone company is deciding whether to use microwave or radio wave signals for their 5G network. Both can transmit data, but microwaves have a shorter wavelength. Evaluate which type of radiation would be better for transmitting large amounts of data over short distances, and explain the trade-off in terms of signal range.
correct_answer: Microwaves are better for short-distance high-data transmission because shorter wavelengths carry more information/have higher frequency. However, microwaves are absorbed more easily by obstacles and have shorter range, while radio waves travel further but carry less data.
explanation: This question requires students to compare two EM waves with different wavelengths for a practical application. Shorter wavelengths (microwaves) allow higher data capacity but worse penetration/range. Longer wavelengths (radio waves) travel further but transmit less data. Students must weigh these competing factors and explain the physics reason behind each property.
